key_improvements: Candidate-set hit rate now 100% (72/72) - the Chile/Semillon fix closed the only gap from iteration 1
P1 whites variety top-1 improved from 25.0% to 33.3% (+2 hits) due to SB promotion for different-countries stems
P3 special variety top-1 improved from 70.8% to 75.0% (+1 hit) due to Cava grapes elevated in non-Champagne leaf
MRR variety improved +2.1pp reflecting better within-candidate-set ranking from SB and Cava promotions
Region metrics unchanged - the three fixes primarily targeted variety ranking, not region ranking

key_findings: Candidate-set hit rate now perfect at 100% (72/72 wines) - every wine has its variety+region somewhere in the prediction set
Top-3 variety accuracy improved to 91.7% (66/72) and top-3 region stable at 93.1% (67/72)
Top-1 variety improved to 54.2% (39/72) from 50.0% (36/72) driven by SB promotion in P1 and Cava promotion in P3
P3 Special remains the strongest paper (75.0% top-1 variety, 75.0% top-1 region) with perfect top-3 and candidate-set coverage
P1 Whites improved most from iteration 1 (variety top-1: 25.0% -> 33.3%) but remains weakest overall
P2 Reds stable at 54.2% top-1 variety - the iteration 2 fixes did not target P2 so no change expected
All three targeted fixes delivered measurable improvements: SB promotion (+2 variety top-1 hits), Chile Semillon (+1 candidate set hit), Cava elevation (+1 variety top-1 hit, improved MRR)
iteration_2_recommendations: P1 Tree: Consider elevating South Africa to STRONG SIGNAL for same-country 3-variety questions (Chenin/Chard/SB trio is nearly unique to SA)
P1 Tree: Elevate Chardonnay higher in Branch 4 mixed-bag rankings - it appeared at rank 4 in 2023 P1 Q2
P1 Tree: Add Muscat/Moschofilero with higher priority in Branch 4 aromatic curveball leaf
P2 Tree: Elevate Tempranillo to top-3 for 'classic European origins' questions
P2 Tree: Elevate Corvina and Nerello Mascalese for Italian same-country tours (both ranked outside top-3 in 2023 P2 Q1)
P3 Tree: Riesling Sekt should be elevated from curveball to PLAUSIBLE in non-Champagne sparkling leaf
Overall: Further MRR gains require better ranking within candidate sets, not expansion

results.question_text: Wines 1-4 are from four different countries and two different grape varieties. 1 & 2 are a pair and 3 & 4 are a pair. Each pair is from the same single grape variety.
results.tree_branch: Branch 1 (same single grape variety within pairs) -> Sub-branch 2-wine same-variety -> Leaf (stem says 'different countries'): STRONG SIGNAL: Chardonnay, Sauvignon Blanc. PLAUSIBLE: Chenin Blanc, Pinot Gris. CURVEBALL: Semillon.
results.reasoning: ITERATION 2 CHANGE: The updated tree has a conditional leaf for 'different countries' that promotes Sauvignon Blanc to STRONG SIGNAL (from PLAUSIBLE in default leaf). The stem explicitly says 'four different countries and two different grape varieties' with pair structure. Applying the NEW 'different countries' leaf: SB is now STRONG SIGNAL alongside Chardonnay. This is a major ranking change from iteration 1 where SB was ranked 2nd - it should now share top-1 position. Additionally, the Semillon region leaf now includes Chile/Maule Valley as PLAUSIBLE. Two variety pairs across four countries: Pair 1 likely SB (cross-country comparison is SB's strongest structure per tree rationale). Pair 2 likely Semillon (CURVEBALL in this leaf). The ageing potential emphasis in the question further supports Semillon (Hunter Valley Semillon ages famously).
